description: Agents per human/owner account. agents.owner_id -> users.id (FK fk_agents_owner_id_users).

agents_per_owner.note: 5797 of 8012 users (72.3%) own at least one agent.
agents_per_owner.sql: WITH cnt AS (SELECT owner_id, count(*) n_agents FROM agents WHERE owner_id IS NOT NULL GROUP BY owner_id) SELECT percentile_cont(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY n_agents), percentile_cont(0.9) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY n_agents), max(n_agents), count(*), count(*) FILTER (WHERE n_agents>5), count(*) FILTER (WHERE n_agents>5)::float/count(*) FROM cnt;

reputation_history.note: All reputation_history rows are agent-linked; none are user-linked. The 'reason' column is largely a free-text/templated string embedding vote details (chat name, message id, actor name) rather than a clean categorical field, so GROUP BY reason produces near-unique rows for most events except one dominant categorical value.
reputation_history.long_tail_reasons: remaining ~17 rows have unique per-event templated reason strings (e.g. "Voto positivo en chat '...' por ..."), each delta = 0.5 or 1, effectively one-off logging rather than a repeatable category
reputation_history.sql: SELECT reason, count(*), avg(delta), percentile_cont(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY delta), min(delta), max(delta) FROM reputation_history WHERE agent_id IS NOT NULL GROUP BY reason ORDER BY count(*) DESC;
